title: Finding your place in the story of Jesus. Part 3
scripture_text: ‘’Now Jesus himself was about thirty years old when he began his ministry. He was the son, so it was thought, of Joseph, the son of Heli, the son of Matthat, the son of Levi, the son of Melki, the son of Jannai, the son of Joseph, the son of Mattathias, the son of Amos, the son of Nahum, the son of Esli, the son of Naggai, the son of Maath, the son of Mattathias, the son of Semein, the son of Josek, the son of Joda, the son of Joanan, the son of Rhesa, the son of Zerubbabel, the son of Shealtiel, the son of Neri the son of Melki, the son of Addi, the son of Cosam, the son of Elmadam, the son of Er, the son of Joshua, the son of Eliezer, the son of Jorim, the son of Matthat, he son of Levi, the son of Simeon the son of Judah, the son of Joseph, the son of Jonam, the son of Eliakim, the son of Melea, the son of Menna, the son of Mattatha, the son of Nathan, the son of David, the son of Jesse, the son of Obed, the son of Boaz, the son of Salmon, the son of Nahshon, the son of Amminadab, the son of Ram the son of Hezron, the son of Perez, the son of Judah, the son of Jacob, the son of Isaac, the son of Abraham, the son of Terah, the son of Nahor, the son of Serug, the son of Reu, the son of Peleg, the son of Eber, the son of Shelah, the son of Cainan, the son of Arphaxad, the son of Shem, the son of Noah, the son of Lamech, the son of Methuselah, the son of Enoch, the son of Jared, the son of Mahalalel, the son of Kenan, the son of Enosh, the son of Seth, the son of Adam, the son of God.’’
devotional:
I find it interesting that Luke begins chapter three by talking about the political and religious power brokers who believed they were the people who really counted, unlike the seemly insignificant Jannai’s and Naggai’s and others who made an eternal difference as they played their role in the Jesus story.

2. The genealogy teaches us that God honors and celebrates our humanity. When Luke wrote his gospel, he was writing to people who had been saved out of Greek and Roman culture. Greeks and Romans had a pantheon of gods that were seen as superhuman, often were cruel and sexually perverse. They were seen as distant and very different to humans. Zeus was the most important god to the Greeks. He was worshipped in every Greek home, and was famous for his promiscuity. Zeus was said to live on top of Mount Olympus with the other major gods. From there, he could see everything that humans did. He sent thunder, lightning, rain, and winds down to Earth.

When we compare Jesus with the common understanding that the Greeks had about ‘gods,’ this genealogy must have blown their minds This was unheard of, where God would come to earth, using the normalities of everyday living. Human body, [Mary], families, history etc. The genealogy of Jesus, teaches us that he is one of us. These early Greek/Gentile Christians may have had to rethink their understanding of God. A God who comes close, a God who understands, a God who shares our experiences.

Listen to this:-

‘Shattering records with their seemingly non-stop output of movies and TV shows, Marvel is the movie franchise that has steadily become the highest-grossing in the world. Among many other high-grossing film franchises in the U.S. and Canada (Harry Potter, James Bond, Lord of the Rings, etc.), Marvel takes first place with a staggering $10.2 billion lead ahead of Star Wars in second place. While this may surprise some, that number doesn’t even include the X-Men or Spider-Man franchises. Thanks to the endless amounts of usable material from generations of comic books, the end of the Marvel universe may never come.’ https://www.ign.com/articles/highest-grossing-marvel-movies.

The reason why I share this is because the Marvel movies, are based on the Greek and Roman mythological gods. It may be that their popularity and success is because these movies reflect the kind of god most people really want. A strong deliverer who is so different to us, so that we can call on him in a crisis, but live perfectly well without him.

The genealogy of Jesus sanctifies the ordinary. It teaches us that God uses ordinary people to bring his redeeming purposes into the world. Yes there were a few notable names in Luke’s list, but as we have seen many were named but unknown.
prayer: Father thank you we are human enough to be part of the story of Jesus
html:
Finding your place in the story of Jesus. Part 3

Study Text: Luke 3:23-38
‘’Now Jesus himself was about thirty years old when he began his ministry. He was the son, so it was thought, of Joseph, the son of Heli, the son of Matthat, the son of Levi, the son of Melki, the son of Jannai, the son of Joseph, the son of Mattathias, the son of Amos, the son of Nahum, the son of Esli, the son of Naggai, the son of Maath, the son of Mattathias, the son of Semein, the son of Josek, the son of Joda, the son of Joanan, the son of Rhesa, the son of Zerubbabel, the son of Shealtiel, the son of Neri the son of Melki, the son of Addi, the son of Cosam, the son of Elmadam, the son of Er, the son of Joshua, the son of Eliezer, the son of Jorim, the son of Matthat, he son of Levi, the son of Simeon the son of Judah, the son of Joseph, the son of Jonam, the son of Eliakim, the son of Melea, the son of Menna, the son of Mattatha, the son of Nathan, the son of David, the son of Jesse, the son of Obed, the son of Boaz, the son of Salmon, the son of Nahshon, the son of Amminadab, the son of Ram the son of Hezron, the son of Perez, the son of Judah, the son of Jacob, the son of Isaac, the son of Abraham, the son of Terah, the son of Nahor, the son of Serug, the son of Reu, the son of Peleg, the son of Eber, the son of Shelah, the son of Cainan, the son of Arphaxad, the son of Shem, the son of Noah, the son of Lamech, the son of Methuselah, the son of Enoch, the son of Jared, the son of Mahalalel, the son of Kenan, the son of Enosh, the son of Seth, the son of Adam, the son of God.’’

I find it interesting that Luke begins chapter three by talking about the political and religious power brokers who believed they were the people who really counted, unlike the seemly insignificant Jannai’s and Naggai’s and others who made an eternal difference as they played their role in the Jesus story.

2. The genealogy teaches us that God honors and celebrates our humanity. When Luke wrote his gospel, he was writing to people who had been saved out of Greek and Roman culture. Greeks and Romans had a pantheon of gods that were seen as superhuman, often were cruel and sexually perverse. They were seen as distant and very different to humans. Zeus was the most important god to the Greeks. He was worshipped in every Greek home, and was famous for his promiscuity. Zeus was said to live on top of Mount Olympus with the other major gods. From there, he could see everything that humans did. He sent thunder, lightning, rain, and winds down to Earth.

When we compare Jesus with the common understanding that the Greeks had about ‘gods,’ this genealogy must have blown their minds This was unheard of, where God would come to earth, using the normalities of everyday living. Human body, [Mary], families, history etc. The genealogy of Jesus, teaches us that he is one of us. These early Greek/Gentile Christians may have had to rethink their understanding of God. A God who comes close, a God who understands, a God who shares our experiences.

Listen to this:-

‘Shattering records with their seemingly non-stop output of movies and TV shows, Marvel is the movie franchise that has steadily become the highest-grossing in the world. Among many other high-grossing film franchises in the U.S. and Canada (Harry Potter, James Bond, Lord of the Rings, etc.), Marvel takes first place with a staggering $10.2 billion lead ahead of Star Wars in second place. While this may surprise some, that number doesn’t even include the X-Men or Spider-Man franchises. Thanks to the endless amounts of usable material from generations of comic books, the end of the Marvel universe may never come.’ https://www.ign.com/articles/highest-grossing-marvel-movies.

The reason why I share this is because the Marvel movies, are based on the Greek and Roman mythological gods. It may be that their popularity and success is because these movies reflect the kind of god most people really want. A strong deliverer who is so different to us, so that we can call on him in a crisis, but live perfectly well without him.

The genealogy of Jesus sanctifies the ordinary. It teaches us that God uses ordinary people to bring his redeeming purposes into the world. Yes there were a few notable names in Luke’s list, but as we have seen many were named but unknown.

Prayer: Father thank you we are human enough to be part of the story of Jesus
Bible in a year reading plan: -Job 16 -18
